qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Event Exposure subscription deletion lacks ownership enforcement and uses predictable IDs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets Event Exposure subscription create/delete ownership logic in `internal/sbi/processor/event_exposure.go`, not the advisory's nil-pointer dereference in callback handling through `internal/sbi/api_httpcallback.go` and `internal/sbi/processor/notifier.go`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable callback path or the nil-pointer dereference root cause in `DataChangeNotificationProcedure`.
evidence: The advisory and patch `65d7070f4bfd016864cbbaefbd506bbc85d2fa92` address `POST /sdm-subscriptions` callback handling by validating `NotifyItems` and `:supi` in `internal/sbi/api_httpcallback.go` and by guarding nil `UdmUeFindBySupi` results in `internal/sbi/processor/notifier.go` before dereferencing `ue.UdmSubsToNotify`.

GitHub issue `free5gc/free5gc#781` shows the crash path is a malformed callback request to `/nudm-sdm/v2/../../sdm-subscriptions...` that reaches `processor.(*Processor).DataChangeNotificationProcedure` and panics with `runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference` at `notifier.go:30`.

The only final qualifying finding, `event_exposure_subscription_delete_bypass`, instead concerns predictable Event Exposure subscription IDs and missing ownership checks on `POST/PATCH/DELETE /nudm-ee/v1/:ueIdentity/ee-subscriptions/:subscriptionId` in `internal/context/context.go` and `internal/sbi/processor/event_exposure.go`.

`artifacts/component_family_checklist.md` also records the callback family tied to `/sdm-subscriptions` as pruned, while the accepted final finding is the separate Event Exposure access-control issue.
